@graph.title: ApplicationInterface
@graph.description: Identifiers for the type of interface for the application.
@graph.creator: Isabella Ruiz

@graph.definition: An application programming interface (API) is a form of interface that allows applications to access the features or data of an operating system, application, or other service. An API may have a required protocol or set of principles. Some examples of protocols are SOAP, XML-RPC and JSON-RPC. An example of an API with a set of principles is REST.
A command-line interface (CLI) is a form of interface where input to an application is provided as lines of text typically within a shell.

A graphical user interface (GUI) is a form of user interface that allows users to interact with an application through graphical icons, forms and other elements with both a keyboard and a pointing device.
